current_gap.user_story: 作为用户，我需要先确认或修改验收标准，才能让 agent 判断任务完成。
current_gap.reason: Acceptance criteria have not been approved by the user yet.

skills.name: nori
skills.description: Root OpenNori router for the complete agent capability bundle. Use when the user says to use or continue OpenNori, asks for autogoal, asks OpenNori to take over an existing AC discussion, asks whether a goal is complete, wants evidence recorded, states required Skills or stack preferences, asks for project health, wants architecture decided first, or expects the agent to use OpenNori without exposing CLI parameters. Treat Plugin discovery, packaged Skills, opennori CLI, and .opennori state as coupled parts of one product.

skills.name: nori-autogoal
skills.description: Automatically converge a rough user idea into a standard OpenNori Nori Contract Draft without creating a separate autogoal artifact. Use when the user asks for autogoal, wants OpenNori to derive goal and AC from a rough idea, or wants fewer clarification rounds before approving a contract.

skills.name: nori-acceptance
skills.description: Discover, draft, approve, and revise human-centered OpenNori acceptance criteria from natural-language goals, fuzzy ideas, Skill-prepared brainstorm directions, next outcomes, or user corrections. Use when completion meaning is unclear, AC quality is being discussed, or a human-facing outcome needs to become a Nori Contract.

skills.name: nori-evidence
skills.description: Record, prune, and judge reviewable OpenNori evidence for acceptance criteria while preserving agent freedom to choose verification methods. Use when the user says to attach a validation, confirms behavior, waives a gap, asks why an AC is passing, or says evidence is stale, invalid, or insufficient.

skills.name: nori-capability-profile
skills.description: Capture and check OpenNori execution preferences such as required Skills, preferred stacks, avoided tools, install policy, and user-declared implementation constraints. Use when the user says a task must use a specific Skill, prefers a library or stack, wants to avoid a tool, or requires approval before installs.

skills.name: nori-architecture-brainstorm
skills.description: Establish a reviewable OpenNori Architecture Baseline before non-trivial implementation. Use when the user wants a good architecture first, asks for built-in or project architecture profiles, prefers a stack that should shape implementation, or expects future agent work to follow a confirmed technical baseline.

skills.name: nori-architecture-apply
skills.description: Apply a confirmed OpenNori Architecture Baseline while implementing acceptance gaps. Use before non-trivial code changes when a current goal has architecture state, when resuming implementation in a new session, or when the agent must ensure Product AC work stays aligned with the baseline.

skills.name: nori-architecture-challenge
skills.description: Create reviewable Architecture Challenges when evidence conflicts with a confirmed OpenNori Architecture Baseline. Use when the baseline appears too heavy, too weak, incompatible with project conventions, blocked by license/security/runtime constraints, or contradicted by implementation evidence.

skills.name: nori-build-vs-buy
skills.description: Record OpenNori build-vs-buy decisions before infrastructure or platform work so agents prefer existing project code, standard libraries, official SDKs, and mature open-source libraries before self-building. Use when implementation touches CLI parsing, schemas, storage, rendering, routing, auth, installers, validators, adapters, UI primitives, or other reusable infrastructure.

skills.name: nori-project-health
skills.description: Diagnose, initialize, upgrade, uninstall, and recover the complete OpenNori capability bundle: Plugin discovery, packaged Skill health, opennori CLI access, project-local .opennori state, manifest, and current-goal integrity. Use when the user asks whether OpenNori is ready, wants setup, sees broken `.opennori` state, or needs safe lifecycle actions with preview and explicit confirmation.

skills.name: nori-reporting
skills.description: Explain OpenNori status, current gaps, completion decisions, user intervention, reports, changes, and context export in human terms. Use when the user asks whether work is complete, what remains, what changed, what they need to do, or how to continue after a completed goal.

requirement.reason: OpenNori self-goal changes architecture requirement routing, persisted state schema, CLI routes, packaged Skills, lifecycle checks, and tests.

apply_records.summary: Architecture Requirement routing keeps subjective non-trivial judgment in Skills and agent-user review, then stores explicit requirement state for deterministic CLI routing.

Acceptance discovery, brainstorm, draft, AC quality review, and next-loop handoff now use Skill-prepared inputs and agent/user judgment; the CLI only validates objective state shape and stores provided data.
Architecture Profile and Baseline now require concrete technical architecture sections while keeping subjective architecture quality in Skills and user review.
Visible interface UI/UX acceptance discovery is implemented as packaged Skill behavior and user-review guidance, not as CLI subjective validation.

Complete product acceptance expansion is implemented in packaged Skill behavior, local development Skills, protocol, README, website, and objective asset tests while keeping subjective AC quality out of CLI hard validators.

Complete-product autogoal coverage self-check is implemented in packaged Skills, local development Skills, protocol, README, website, and objective asset tests without adding CLI subjective validators.
Autogoal was implemented as packaged Skill-driven convergence into the existing Nori Contract Draft path, without adding a separate Autogoal Contract or MVP workflow.

High-risk evidence handling now preserves objective evidence results while surfacing confidence and evidence_health review risks; the CLI does not use fixed strong/weak natural-language evidence lists to rewrite subjective sufficiency.
CLI boundary audit kept subjective product semantics in Skills and narrowed hard validation to objective contract, ledger, evidence, architecture, lifecycle, and schema integrity.
Conversation-to-contract adoption is implemented as Skill-driven nori-acceptance behavior backed by the existing standard draft path, without adding a new CLI workflow or autogoal artifact.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: 将 acceptance discovery、brainstorm 和 draft generation 从 CLI 入口拆到领域模块，同时把主观 AC 质量审查留给 Skills 和用户确认
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Acceptance discovery、brainstorm 和 draft generation 是 OpenNori 的领域逻辑，当前由 src/acceptance.ts 承载；主观 AC 质量不再由 CLI 词表判定，而由 packaged Skills 要求 agent 与用户复核。
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/acceptance.ts 承载 BRAINSTORM_CANDIDATES、DEFAULT_CRITERIA、DISCOVERY_GAPS 和 draft helpers；src/cli/commands/acceptance.ts 负责命令入口；test/core.test.js 与 test/cli-commands.test.js 只覆盖 discovery/brainstorm/draft 的客观状态读写，不测试自然语言 AC 质量判断。
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node.js ESM 模块足够承载常量和纯函数；无需新增运行时依赖。
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: 无官方 SDK 适用；这是 OpenNori 产品领域规则。
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: 成熟 NLP/validation 库会增加复杂度，也不能替代 agent/user 对人类视角 AC 的语义判断；TK 参考强调 Skill/产品面和确定性核心分离。
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: brainstorm/discover/draft 的结构化读写规则小且属于 OpenNori 核心语义；主观 AC 质量判断不在自研 CLI 范围内，应沉淀到 Skills 行为规则和用户确认。

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Prevent concurrent agent or tool writes from losing OpenNori active-goal evidence ledger updates.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use a small project-local write lock around active-goal mutating CLI commands so read-modify-write operations on .opennori/active are serialized.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/cli/command-tree.ts already centralizes command execution policy, and src/cli/runtime.ts already owns active-goal loading and saving; the lock belongs at this CLI runtime boundary.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node fs.mkdirSync provides atomic directory creation that works as a local filesystem lock for this single-host project-state use case.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official SDK applies to OpenNori local state consistency.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: proper-lockfile and lockfile-style libraries were considered, but this need is a narrow local single-writer guard around one project-state directory; adding a dependency would increase product surface without improving the acceptance loop.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: The implementation is small and uses atomic mkdir plus stale-lock cleanup; it protects the user-visible evidence ledger without introducing a general locking framework.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: 让 opennori check 同时暴露 active goal 的 Architecture Baseline 健康和恢复建议
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: architecture_check 是 OpenNori 自有验收状态语义：它复用现有 architectureState、agent surface 和 active goal 恢复逻辑，把 baseline missing/draft/invalid/challenged/stale surface 作为 check warnings 暴露给 agent 和用户。
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: 已有 src/architecture/state.ts 的 architectureState、src/lifecycle/doctor.ts 与 manifest health、src/acceptance.ts 的 acceptance_review surface；src/cli/commands/check.ts 聚合 active contract 状态。
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node.js 标准库足够读取本地 .opennori/ 与 agent route 文件；不需要额外 runtime。
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: 无官方 SDK；这是 OpenNori 产品状态语义。
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: 通用 schema/validator 库能校验 JSON 形状，但不能表达 OpenNori 的 Product AC 与 Architecture Baseline 分离、challenge、agent surface 恢复建议。
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: 逻辑小、可测试、依赖 OpenNori 内部状态对象；复用现有 architectureState 比引入外部 validator 更贴合产品边界。后续若 baseline/profile schema 复杂化，再评估 JSON Schema/Zod 等库。

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Expose reviewable Architecture Profile details before baseline confirmation
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Reuse OpenNori's existing architecture profile state and CLI JSON surface to expose applies_to, sources, principles, checks, preferred libraries, avoid boundaries, and build-vs-buy policy before baseline confirmation.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/architecture/profile.ts owns built-in and project Architecture Profiles; src/cli/commands/architecture/profile.ts and profiles.ts expose opennori architecture profile review.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node fs/path are sufficient for reading project-local profile JSON.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official SDK applies to OpenNori product-state semantics.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: Registry/schema libraries would be heavier than needed for this deterministic profile review surface; schema validation can be reconsidered if profile schemas grow.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: Architecture Profile review is OpenNori product semantics and should reuse the existing Architecture Baseline domain model without adding a generic registry dependency.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Keep the growing OpenNori command surface maintainable with a TypeScript command layer
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: OpenNori uses citty command definitions in TypeScript modules while keeping deterministic JSON output and a thin opennori entrypoint.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/cli/command-tree.ts defines the citty command tree, src/cli/commands/** owns command modules, src/cli.ts is a thin entrypoint, and tests cover command behavior through module calls and the real bin.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node can expose process.argv but does not provide a maintainable nested command definition model for the growing CLI surface.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official OpenNori SDK applies; the relevant ecosystem choice is a mature CLI library.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: citty 0.2.2 MIT matches the confirmed baseline and is used by modern TypeScript tooling patterns; commander and cac remain fallback candidates only if concrete packaging or nested-command evidence challenges citty.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Provide a local visual dashboard transport that observes OpenNori contract, gap, evidence, architecture, and live agent activity without becoming an agent runtime.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use Hono plus @hono/node-server for the local dashboard HTTP/SSE transport while keeping OpenNori's .opennori state, event ledger, activity, and snapshot projection as first-party domain modules.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori already has TypeScript domain modules, citty CLI commands, dashboard static assets, and separate src/kernel/activity.ts, src/kernel/events.ts, and src/kernel/snapshot.ts modules. The previous hand-written HTTP server was becoming routing, static-file, SSE, and error-response infrastructure rather than OpenNori domain logic.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node http, fs, path, url, and child_process can implement the transport, but keeping route matching, response handling, SSE framing, server startup, and error handling as custom code repeats solved infrastructure and makes later dashboard growth more expensive.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: @hono/node-server is Hono's official Node adapter and runs Hono apps on Node.js using web-standard Request/Response APIs. Hono's streamSSE helper provides a typed SSE response boundary; package-root static asset resolution remains OpenNori-owned because Hono's serve-static root is cwd-relative.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: Hono is a lightweight TypeScript web framework already used in mature agent-adjacent stacks such as opencode's TypeScript server layer. Express is older and heavier for this local kernel, while Fastify is better for larger API services but adds plugin/server architecture beyond the current observation surface.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Provide a local visual dashboard that observes OpenNori contract, gap, evidence, architecture, and live agent activity without becoming an agent runtime.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use Node standard library HTTP plus Server-Sent Events for a local loopback observation kernel. OpenNori keeps .opennori as source of truth, emits a small durable JSONL event ledger, projects current snapshot JSON, and serves a static SVG dashboard. MCP remains a later structured agent tool surface, not the dashboard transport.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori already has TypeScript domain modules, citty CLI commands, JSON .opennori state, manifest-managed lifecycle, and package-local static assets; no existing kernel/dashboard layer is present.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node http, fs, path, url, and child_process are sufficient for a loopback server, JSON snapshot endpoint, static file serving, and SSE event stream.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official OpenNori SDK exists for dashboard transport. MCP is useful later for agent tool calls, but OpenAI/Codex Skills plus CLI already cover current agent state writes; MCP is not required to stream a human dashboard.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: Express/Fastify/Hono and frontend frameworks were considered, but the first dashboard is local, unauthenticated loopback, static, and has three endpoints; adding a server or SPA framework would increase package surface before routing complexity exists.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: The self-built portion is intentionally small adapter glue around Node's standard library and OpenNori's own state semantics. External libraries would not replace the product-specific event, activity, and snapshot projection model.
build_vs_buy_decisions.superseded_reason: OpenNori's dashboard transport should establish a long-term framework boundary early. Hono provides reusable routing, response, and SSE helpers while OpenNori keeps .opennori state, event ledger, activity, and snapshot projection as first-party domain logic.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Provide a productized local visual dashboard that helps users observe OpenNori agent activity, current acceptance gap, user intervention, architecture health, and completion decision without becoming an agent runtime or process log.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use React, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, Radix primitives, Motion, and Lucide React for the dashboard UI while keeping Hono as the local snapshot/SSE kernel and .opennori as the source of truth.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori already has a Hono dashboard kernel, /api/snapshot, /api/events, TypeScript modules, and a small vanilla dashboard. The vanilla files prove the transport but are not a long-term UI architecture for live visual state, motion, responsive layout, or accessible overlays.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Browser DOM, CSS, SVG, and EventSource can render the dashboard, but hand-rolling component state, animation, responsive primitives, accessible dialogs/tooltips/tabs, and build output would repeat solved frontend infrastructure.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official OpenNori UI SDK exists. Vite is the official modern build path for React projects through @vitejs/plugin-react, and @tailwindcss/vite is Tailwind's Vite integration.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: React is a mature UI library used broadly in agent UI projects; Vite is a mature ESM build tool; Tailwind CSS v4 provides utility styling and tokens; Radix primitives provide accessible UI behavior without a heavy visual system; Motion provides React animation; Lucide React provides icons. TK references show mature agent UI projects separate daemon/kernel state from frontend UI and often use React/Vite/Tailwind-style stacks.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Parse OpenNori generated acceptance and report markdown only where users or agents may edit reviewable documents
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: OpenNori keeps JSON evidence and contract files authoritative and uses Markdown primarily as generated review surface. The current tiny parser only recovers the known OpenNori acceptance table shape, so it can remain local for now; if user-edited Markdown becomes authoritative, micromark/remark/frontmatter libraries must be re-evaluated before expanding parsing.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: Current project writes structured JSON as source of truth and has one local parseAcceptanceMarkdown helper for generated acceptance markdown.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node has no Markdown or frontmatter parser.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official OpenNori Markdown SDK exists; CommonMark-compatible libraries are the relevant ecosystem option.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: micromark 4.0.2 MIT was checked on npm on 2026-06-12; remark and gray-matter are follow-up candidates if frontmatter/AST editing becomes product scope.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: Self-build is limited to parsing OpenNori's own generated table as a recovery/review helper; it is not a general Markdown parser. This boundary avoids adding parser dependencies until Markdown becomes authoritative user input.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Warn when a complete-looking OpenNori goal relies on stale, summary-only, or bulk-template evidence
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Reuse OpenNori's existing evidence ledger fields to audit evidence health without forcing adapters: created_at, sources, reviewability, limitations, basis, confidence, and summary patterns are enough for first-pass warnings.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/core/evidence.ts normalizes evidence and evidence health; src/core/report.ts plus src/cli/commands/check.ts expose evidence metadata in report/check surfaces.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node built-ins are sufficient; no parser or database is needed.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official SDK applies to OpenNori's evidence semantics.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: Generic validation libraries would not understand OpenNori's human acceptance evidence semantics; schema libraries can be revisited if evidence schemas grow.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: Evidence health is OpenNori product-domain semantics. The audit should stay flexible and warn on review risk without turning evidence into a fixed adapter taxonomy.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Preserve multiple reviewable evidence sources on evidence add while using citty command definitions.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Keep citty as the command framework and use a narrow command-local repeated-source collector for evidence source flags because citty does not expose repeatable string args.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: src/cli/commands/evidence/add.ts already uses citty command definitions; src/cli/commands/evidence/source-parsing.ts is limited to translating evidence source flags into EvidenceSource objects.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node util.parseArgs can expose duplicate tokens, but using it directly here would keep a second command parser path after the citty migration.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official SDK applies to OpenNori evidence CLI ergonomics.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: citty 0.2.2 supports string, boolean, enum, and positional args but not repeatable array args; commander/yargs could support variadic/repeatable options but would replace the confirmed citty baseline rather than solve this local gap.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: The local collector is less than a parser framework: it scans exact evidence source flags and preserves multi-source evidence without changing the CLI architecture or adding a second dependency.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Ship OpenNori Skills as Codex Plugin package assets instead of hard-coded JavaScript strings or project-local copies
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use the OpenAI/Codex Skill directory shape as the source of truth: plugins/opennori/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, exposed through plugins/opennori/.codex-plugin/plugin.json and the repo marketplace, then read by a thin runtime module for Plugin state, manifest, and doctor checks.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori ships .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, plugins/opennori/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, and package-local plugins/opennori/skills/*/SKILL.md assets; src/skills.ts reads package assets and src/plugin.ts reports Plugin state without copying Skills into user projects.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node fs/path can discover and read package-local Skill assets; no template engine is needed for static Skill content.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: OpenAI/Codex Skill authoring convention is directory-based SKILL.md with optional references/scripts/assets, which defines the asset shape even though no SDK is needed to read local files.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: superpowers, vibecode-pro-max-kit, ECC, and compound-engineering-plugin demonstrate directory Skill assets and plugin/component packaging boundaries; OpenNori borrows the asset shape without adopting process-centered install/sync.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: The local reader is small OpenNori product glue around first-party Skill content; generic plugin registries or template systems would add maintenance and package surface without improving the acceptance loop.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Validate OpenNori persisted state with public JSON Schema and runtime Ajv checks
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use package-local schemas/*.schema.json as public protocol structure and Ajv 2020 runtime validation for manifest, active evidence payloads, Architecture Baseline, and build-vs-buy decisions; keep OpenNori semantic rules in domain validators.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori already writes JSON state under .opennori and has validateContract, architecture health, doctor, and build-vs-buy semantic checks; this slice adds structural validation without replacing those domain rules.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: JSON.parse validates syntax only and cannot express required object shape, enum values, or array item structure.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: No official OpenNori SDK exists; JSON Schema is the relevant open standard for shareable protocol structure.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: Ajv is already selected in package.json and supports JSON Schema 2020-12; Zod/Valibot remain alternatives for TypeScript-first authoring but are not the public protocol format.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Provide one explicit OpenNori setup entry that installs the Codex Plugin, packaged Skills, global opennori CLI, project .opennori state, and doctor check without making users understand separate plugin/npm/bootstrap paths.
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use the official codex plugin marketplace/add commands for Plugin registration, npm global install for the CLI binary, and existing OpenNori lifecycle install/doctor modules for project state. Self-built code is limited to preview/confirm orchestration and command generation.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: OpenNori already has citty command modules, lifecycle install plans, doctor checks, Plugin package assets, and preview/confirm semantics for project writes.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node child_process can run confirmed external commands and fs/path can inspect package.json and .opennori state, but Node does not provide Codex Plugin registration or npm package installation semantics.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: Codex exposes codex plugin marketplace add and codex plugin add as the official registration surface; npm/npx provide package execution and global CLI installation instead of postinstall side effects.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: TK references show plugin/skill bundle distribution and manifest-managed install boundaries; no extra installer framework is needed for this narrow explicit setup orchestration.
build_vs_buy_decisions.self_build_reason: Only the OpenNori-specific setup plan schema and preview/confirm orchestration are self-built; plugin registration and CLI installation are delegated to official CLIs.

build_vs_buy_decisions.need: Run TypeScript source entrypoints during local development without making the published OpenNori CLI depend on a development loader
build_vs_buy_decisions.summary: Use Node 22 native TypeScript stripping for the repository-local source wrapper and TypeScript rewriteRelativeImportExtensions for emitted JavaScript, while the published opennori binary remains tsc-emitted JavaScript under dist.
build_vs_buy_decisions.current_project: package.json already uses TypeScript, tsc no-bundle builds, NodeNext ESM, and a local bin wrapper; package bin points at dist for npm users.
build_vs_buy_decisions.standard_library: Node 22 exposes native TypeScript stripping for erasable syntax, which can execute OpenNori source entrypoints during development without a third-party loader; standard Node still runs emitted JavaScript in published packages.
build_vs_buy_decisions.official_sdk: The relevant official runtime capability is Node native TypeScript stripping plus tsc emit with rewriteRelativeImportExtensions; no custom loader or package runtime SDK is needed.
build_vs_buy_decisions.open_source: tsx 4.22.4 was evaluated but rejected because its esbuild 0.28.0 dependency produced npm audit high-severity findings in this project; use no additional open-source loader for this boundary.

build_vs_buy.summary: Every recorded build-vs-buy decision includes reviewable reuse candidates and any self-build reason.
